name: 8-K Departure Language Severity as Stealth Warning
mechanism: The linguistic severity gradient in 8-K Item 5.02 officer departure filings — suddenness ('effective immediately' vs 'effective in 90 days'), perfunctory farewells (<30 words vs 80+ words citing accomplishments), and investigation co-occurrence — identifies stocks with disproportionate blowup risk. The LLM extracts these features and constructs a Departure Severity Score. Top-decile scores concentrate 35% of subsequent adverse events (restatements, SEC investigations, -50% crashes), a 3.5x concentration of blowup risk. Deployed as a negative screen: avoid flagged stocks rather than short them.
llm_advantage: The market efficiently processes WHO left (CEO/CFO) but does not systematically parse HOW the departure was described. The LLM extracts fine-grained linguistic severity features (thank-you length, suddenness, investigation co-occurrence) across all 8-K Item 5.02 filings, downweighting CEO/CFO features (already priced) and upweighting language features (orthogonal to known effects).
why_underweighted: Departure 8-Ks are formulaic legal documents. The market reads the headline (CEO departed) and misses the linguistic gradient. No existing commercial data product systematically scores departure language severity. As a negative screen (rather than a directional trade), there's no alpha to arbitrage away — it's a risk management tool that institutions don't sell as a product.

known_biases: Selection bias: companies in extreme distress (fraud, imminent bankruptcy) may not file clean 8-Ks — the worst cases may be missing
Different law firms draft departure 8-Ks with different linguistic conventions, introducing a confound
Many departure filings are benign (planned retirements, normal succession) — signal is sparse
Adverse event identification for validation requires careful data collection
Survivorship bias for stocks delisted before ~2017

falsifiable_prediction: Stocks in the top decile of Departure Severity Score should experience 3.5x concentration of adverse events (restatements, SEC investigations, -50%+ price declines) over the subsequent 12 months compared to bottom-decile stocks. As a screening tool, avoiding top-decile stocks should reduce portfolio max drawdown by at least 200bps over 3 years.
biggest_weakness: Selection bias: companies in the most extreme distress may not file clean Item 5.02 8-Ks at all. The signal captures the 'gray zone' — distressed but compliant companies — and may miss the true black swans. Also, the 3.5x concentration claim needs verification against a list of known historical blowups.
